title: Stress Related

jokes.text: I think it's too good to be wasted on the hired help.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv shows off healthy food she's prepared for the family
jokes.punchline: Hilary's oblivious classist comment about Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary obliviousness and privilege, well-integrated character beat

jokes.text: Don't sweat me, Aunt Viv. Don't sweat me. Don't sweat me.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv compliments Will on looking nice for his first day
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive repetition suggests he's nervous despite acting cool
jokes.explanation: Character-specific nervous energy from Will, repetition adds comedic effect

jokes.text: Carlton wrestling with Will over argyle socks
jokes.setup: Carlton needs his socks for a job interview
jokes.punchline: Visual of two grown men fighting over argyle socks
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that plays to Carlton's uptight nature and sets up his job anxiety

jokes.text: Probably ain't gonna help my foot fungus anyway.
jokes.setup: Will gives back Carlton's precious argyle socks
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has foot fungus, making the socks disgusting
jokes.explanation: Perfect gross-out escalation that retroactively makes Carlton's desperate sock fight even funnier

jokes.text: Carlton's disgusted reaction and 'Keep them'
jokes.setup: Will reveals his foot fungus contamination
jokes.punchline: Carlton's horror at realizing what he was fighting for
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff reaction that completes the sock sequence

jokes.text: Has anyone seen my black leotard? / That ain't funny.
jokes.setup: Ashley innocently asks about her leotard
jokes.punchline: Will's reaction suggests someone (likely Uncle Phil) wearing it
jokes.explanation: Economical visual gag that plays on Uncle Phil's size and the family dynamics

jokes.text: I'm sure all the kids at school wanna know what an assistant talent coordinator does.
jokes.setup: Will wants to speak at Career Day about his job
jokes.punchline: Ashley's polite but cutting observation about his unimpressive title
jokes.explanation: Ashley's innocent but sharp wit deflates Will's ego perfectly

jokes.text: Well, what do they do? / What do they do?
jokes.setup: Ashley questions Will's job title
jokes.punchline: Will realizes he doesn't actually know what his job entails
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's embarrassment, shows his inexperience

jokes.text: I'm gonna get back to you on that, Ash.
jokes.setup: Will doesn't know what his own job involves
jokes.punchline: His attempt to save face with formal deflection
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt at professional language to cover his ignorance

jokes.text: Family's mass exodus from healthy breakfast
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv serves healthy breakfast
jokes.punchline: Everyone suddenly claims to be sick or need to eat elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom group reaction gag, sets up the healthy eating subplot

jokes.text: And my nose is so clogged up I can't even smell my Obsession.
jokes.setup: Hilary describes her cold symptoms
jokes.punchline: Her biggest concern is not being able to smell her expensive perfume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary vanity and materialism, specific brand name makes it funnier

jokes.text: Mr. Dipple? / No, no. Not Dipple. Stimple. / That's what I said. Dipple.
jokes.setup: Will mentions Mr. Stimple
jokes.punchline: Hilary's sick brain can't process the name correctly
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective confusion gag that sets up Hilary's impaired state

jokes.text: Will's nervous stuttering trying to talk to people
jokes.setup: Will trying to be proactive on his first day
jokes.punchline: His nervousness makes him completely unable to communicate
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's fish-out-of-water status in professional environment

jokes.text: Rawr. Great. Ha, ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Werner calls Will a tiger
jokes.punchline: Will's awkward attempt to play along with tiger impression
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy as Will tries too hard to impress his boss

jokes.text: Actually, I need you to move those boxes. / Boxes.
jokes.setup: Will expects important responsibility after his enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: The deflating revelation that it's just moving boxes
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Will's expectations, Will's repeated 'Boxes' sells the disappointment

jokes.text: Will's dejected 'Boxes.'
jokes.setup: Will expected important assignment
jokes.punchline: His deflated delivery of 'Boxes'
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Will's disappointment conveyed in single word delivery.

jokes.text: Body language? / Sure. They gave me the wink and the gun.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims the bank's body language was clear
jokes.punchline: His misinterpretation of finger guns as positive signals
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific Carlton delusion, the finger gun gesture is perfectly in character and quotable

jokes.text: When they give you the wink and the gun that means: 'Hey, you're our man.'
jokes.setup: Carlton's finger gun misinterpretation
jokes.punchline: His confident explanation of this completely wrong interpretation
jokes.explanation: Escalates the delusion with Carlton's trademark misplaced confidence

jokes.text: Listen, after I got finished changing light bulbs and moving boxes... they gave me something important to do. I moved a big box.
jokes.setup: Will builds up to revealing his important task
jokes.punchline: The 'promotion' was just moving a bigger box
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that subverts expectations perfectly, great callback structure

jokes.text: Dag. Who was your boss? Mr. Slate?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions working before Xerox machines
jokes.punchline: Will references Fred Flintstone's boss, suggesting Phil worked in the Stone Age
jokes.explanation: Smart pop culture reference that plays on both Phil's age and Will's street smarts

jokes.text: He was on The Flintstones.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's confused reaction to the Mr. Slate reference
jokes.punchline: Will explaining the reference deadpan
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-up that completes the generational gap joke

jokes.text: What is this? / Tofu and rice cakes. / I tried to warn you. / Mrs. Banks has run amuck.
jokes.setup: Phil expecting his usual sandwich
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dramatic description of Aunt Viv's health crusade
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's formal language makes Aunt Viv sound like a military threat

jokes.text: Gone, sir. / My pimento loaf? / I'm afraid so, sir. / But I still have some bologna in the... / She got that too, sir.
jokes.setup: Phil hoping some junk food survived
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey revealing Aunt Viv's complete victory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the scope of Aunt Viv's kitchen purge

jokes.text: Hey, look, Uncle Phil, what do you say... we slide down to Mickey D's before she clean them out too.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv has eliminated all junk food from the house
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting she might somehow get to McDonald's too
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that plays up Aunt Viv's thoroughness and Will's street language

jokes.text: Ordered? By whom? / Alex Trebek.
jokes.setup: Phil asking who ordered the potential juror to be impartial
jokes.punchline: Random reference to Jeopardy host
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and specific, shows the quality of people Phil has to deal with

jokes.text: Well, Mr. Mitchell Conklin... I'm your friend.
jokes.setup: Guest shared heartbreaking story about having no friends
jokes.punchline: Hilary's patronizing and self-serving response
jokes.explanation: Shows Hilary's inability to be genuinely empathetic even when trying

jokes.text: It's done, Hil. I just... I just went and I got her some medicine. You know, I took a little initiative.
jokes.setup: Will being proactive about Hilary's sickness
jokes.punchline: His proud use of Uncle Phil's advice about 'initiative'
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Will taking Phil's advice to heart

jokes.text: Don't ever touch me.
jokes.setup: Will trying to be helpful and friendly
jokes.punchline: Hilary's cold boundary setting while sick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary selfishness even when Will is helping her

jokes.text: Because I could always tell if someone was guilty just by looking at them. / Sir, even I can't tell by looking. / And they let you be a judge.
jokes.setup: Juror claims to have supernatural guilt detection
jokes.punchline: His insulting response to Phil's reasonable correction
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the absurd people Phil has to deal with as a judge

jokes.text: My brain is all clouded. My head's all fogged up. And I can't think straight. / And now you've got a cold on top of it.
jokes.setup: Hilary describing her cold symptoms
jokes.punchline: Will implying those are her normal symptoms
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and misdirection, classic Will roast of Hilary's intelligence

jokes.text: Here you go, Catwoman.
jokes.setup: Will giving Hilary medicine
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to Catwoman (likely due to her appearance or behavior)
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that likely plays on Hilary's appearance when sick

jokes.text: Say what you will, but he is a good provider.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv criticizing Uncle Phil's eating habits
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's understated defense of his employer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey loyalty with understated British humor

jokes.text: Damn you and your excuses.
jokes.setup: Carlton is stressed about waiting for the bank's call
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate outburst at Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton cracking under pressure, uncharacteristic rudeness

jokes.text: Touch that and you'll be on the first thing smoking back to London.
jokes.setup: Carlton sits down to relax
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's stern territorial warning about the furniture
jokes.explanation: Great Geoffrey assertiveness with colorful threatening language

jokes.text: Carlton Banks here. Oh, hi, Dad. I thought it was someone important.
jokes.setup: Carlton answering the phone with professional importance
jokes.punchline: Dismissing his own father as unimportant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton self-importance and obliviousness about his rudeness

jokes.text: Use only before bedtime. Do not operate heavy machinery. / Good thing we're not on a tractor, huh?
jokes.setup: Will reading medicine warning labels
jokes.punchline: His relief about the specific tractor warning
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about medication warnings

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. Pimple? Bring him in. / No. It's Mr. Stimple. / Bring him in too. The more the merrier.
jokes.setup: Hilary still can't get Stimple's name right
jokes.punchline: She thinks they're two different people now
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the name confusion, now multiplied by drug effects

jokes.text: I'm a little teapot
jokes.setup: Will trying to get Hilary to walk off the medication
jokes.punchline: Hilary breaking into the children's song
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific drug behavior, great visual comedy potential

jokes.text: Hilary go night-night.
jokes.setup: Hilary completely drugged before important sponsor meeting
jokes.punchline: Talks like a toddler about going to sleep
jokes.explanation: Peak of drugged Hilary sequence - baby talk at professional crisis moment creates good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: No. No. Carlton, it's Career Day. You don't even have a job.
jokes.setup: Carlton volunteering to speak at Career Day
jokes.punchline: Ashley's brutal reality check
jokes.explanation: Ashley cutting through Carlton's delusions with surgical precision

jokes.text: Buck up? Buck off.
jokes.setup: Carlton getting rejected by the bank
jokes.punchline: His angry twist on his own earlier advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and character moment, Carlton's polite persona cracking under pressure

jokes.text: How you doing? I'm Hilary's hair designer, Diante.
jokes.setup: Will desperately trying to hide drugged Hilary from sponsor
jokes.punchline: His improvised flamboyant hair stylist persona
jokes.explanation: Creative improvisation under pressure, sets up extended sequence

jokes.text: Oh, excuse me. I'm doing girlfriend's hair. She is so excited to meet you. Just later.
jokes.setup: Will maintaining his stylist character
jokes.punchline: His affected speech patterns and protective behavior
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the impersonation, building the character

jokes.text: Around here we call her, Ricki-Oprah-hue.
jokes.setup: Sponsor asking about Hilary's interviewing style
jokes.punchline: Will's made-up combination of talk show hosts
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau combining three talk show hosts into one nonsense name

jokes.text: Oh, no, you don't. Honey, it took me an hour to do them nails.
jokes.setup: Stimple trying to shake hands
jokes.punchline: Will protecting Hilary's nails as a hair stylist would
jokes.explanation: Good character commitment, shows Will staying in character under pressure

jokes.text: You a Leo, ain't you?
jokes.setup: Will continuing his stylist persona
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical hair stylist astrology reading
jokes.explanation: Plays into hair stylist stereotypes, maintains the character

jokes.text: Well, I was previously in expeditious comestible management. And currently, I'm overseeing a myriad of options.
jokes.setup: Students asking Carlton what he does
jokes.punchline: His pompous way of saying he worked in fast food and is unemployed
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Carlton pretentious language to disguise embarrassing reality, brilliant wordplay

jokes.text: Do you even have a job? / Well, strictly speaking, l... / He's a bum just like my Uncle Eddie.
jokes.setup: Carlton's evasive fancy language
jokes.punchline: Kid cutting through the BS with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Carlton's pretensions by honest kid

jokes.text: Carlton's dark rant about sharks, guppies, and rotting milk
jokes.setup: Carlton being called out by kids
jokes.punchline: His bitter, inappropriate rant destroying children's optimism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark turn showing Carlton's complete breakdown, inappropriate and memorable

jokes.text: You know, with a touch of henna and a kiss of ash blond... you're gonna need yourself a beeper, girl.
jokes.setup: Will continuing his stylist act
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Mr. Stimple would be so attractive he'd need a pager for all the attention
jokes.explanation: Good character work with 90s technology reference (beeper)

jokes.text: You know, with a touch of henna and a kiss of ash blond... you're gonna need yourself a beeper, girl.
jokes.setup: Will still playing hairdresser to sponsor
jokes.punchline: Implies sponsor will be so attractive he'll need communication device for dates
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of hairdresser character - the beeper reference dates it but works for the character.

jokes.text: Will? I thought you were Diante? / Diante?
jokes.setup: Hilary waking up confused during Will's impersonation
jokes.punchline: The charade completely falling apart
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as the elaborate deception unravels

jokes.text: I got nighttime-so-you-could-sleep instead of daytime-so-you-could-think medicine. Man, how is she supposed to drive a tractor?
jokes.setup: Will trying to explain the situation to Stimple
jokes.punchline: Callback to the tractor joke while explaining Hilary's condition
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that ties together the episode's medication subplot

jokes.text: With only one moronic cousin running around... you're way ahead of the game.
jokes.setup: Stimple learning about Will's family connection to Hilary
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment about family nepotism
jokes.explanation: Clever business humor about nepotism that actually saves the situation

jokes.text: Uh, see, well, you bobbed when you should've weaved.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking how Will ended up looking like he fought someone
jokes.punchline: Will's boxing explanation for his appearance
jokes.explanation: Boxing metaphor for whatever physical comedy happened off-screen

jokes.text: Last one to Roscoe's Chicken and Waffles ain't eating.
jokes.setup: Family struggling with Aunt Viv's healthy food
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil leading the charge to junk food restaurant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil character moment and resolution of the health food subplot

jokes.text: They gave me the wink and the gun. They gave me the wink and the gun.
jokes.setup: Carlton's final dejected state
jokes.punchline: His repeated, pathetic clinging to the misunderstood gesture
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking and funny callback that shows Carlton's complete defeat and delusion

jokes.text: Buck up, pal.
jokes.setup: Carlton's defeated repetition of his delusion
jokes.punchline: Will using Carlton's own earlier advice against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback that brings Carlton's arc full circle with ironic reversal

tmdb_overview: After learning a little something about initiative, Will jumps to Hilary's aid prior to a key business meeting at the station, meanwhile an unemployed Carlton subs for Philip on career day at Ashley's school.
